content:
$$ \text { Match the Column I with Column II } $$

Column I
(Animals)		 Column II
(Respiratory organs)
i. Sponges a. External gills
ii. Insects b. Plasma membrane
iii. Limulus c. Tracheae
iv. Amphibian tadpoles d. Book gills

Select the correct option

options.content: $\mathrm{i}-\mathrm{b}$ ii - c iii - d iv - a
i - b ii - c iii - a iv - d
i - c ii - b iii - d iv - a
i - c ii - b iii -a iv - d

explanation:
• i. Sponges: Sponges are simple multicellular animals that lack specialized respiratory organs. Gas exchange occurs directly between individual cells and the surrounding water through diffusion across the plasma membrane.

    • Therefore, i matches with b. Plasma membrane.

  • ii. Insects: Insects possess a highly efficient respiratory system consisting of a network of tubes called tracheae that open to the outside via spiracles. These tracheae directly deliver oxygen to the tissues.

    • Therefore, ii matches with c. Tracheae.

  • iii. Limulus (Horseshoe crab): Limulus is an aquatic arthropod (chelicerate) that breathes using specialized respiratory structures called book gills. These are leaf-like appendages arranged like the pages of a book.

    • Therefore, iii matches with d. Book gills.

  • iv. Amphibian tadpoles: Amphibian tadpoles are larval amphibians that live in water. They breathe using external gills, which are feathery structures located on the sides of their heads, allowing them to absorb oxygen from the water.

    • Therefore, iv matches with a. External gills.

Combining these matches, we get:

i - b

ii - c

iii - d

iv - a

This matches Option A.

The final answer is $\boxed{\text{A}}$

content: Which one of the following statements is INCORRECT?

options.content: Cnidaria is the first animal group showing true simple nervous system.
Diffused type of nervous system is the first landmark in the development of nervous system.
In plants, control and co-ordination mechanism is brought about by sending chemical signals for chemotactic, phototropic and thigmotactic movements.
Ladder type of nervous system is found in sponges and Hydra.

explanation:
Let us carefully examine each option step by step:

Option A:

Cnidaria is the first animal group showing true simple nervous system.

✔ This is correct. Cnidarians (like Hydra, Jellyfish) have a diffused nerve net, which is the simplest form of nervous system.

Option B:

Diffused type of nervous system is the first landmark in the development of nervous system.

✔ This is also correct, because the nerve net in Cnidarians represents the earliest form of nervous system organization.

Option C:

In plants, control and co-ordination mechanism is brought about by sending chemical signals for chemotactic, phototropic and thigmotactic movements.

✔ This is correct. Plants do not have a nervous system. They use plant hormones (like auxins) as chemical signals to show directional movements, e.g. phototropism (towards light), thigmotropism (touch), etc.

Option D:

Ladder type of nervous system is found in sponges and Hydra.

✘ This is incorrect.

  • Sponges have no nervous system at all.

  • Hydra has a diffused nerve net.

  • The ladder type nervous system is actually found in Platyhelminthes (e.g. Planaria).

✅ Answer: Option D

options.content: earthworm
flatworms
roundworm
ringworm

explanation:
Parenchymal circulation is the type of circulation in which the absorbed nutrients and digested food materials pass directly from cell to cell through the fluid-filled body spaces, without the help of blood vessels.

  • In flatworms (phylum Platyhelminthes), there are no specialized blood vessels.

  • They have a solid body with branched digestive cavity, and the digested food moves through the body spaces (parenchyma) by diffusion.

  • This is termed parenchymal circulation.

So, the correct answer is:

Option B – flatworms ✅

content:
Complete the following analogy with respect to classification of mammals.

$$ \text { Marsupials : (i) :: monotremes : (ii) } $$

options.content:
(i) (ii)
$$
\text { pouched mammals }
$$		 $$
\text {  placental mammals }
$$

(i) (ii)
$$
\text { pouched mammals }
$$		 $$
\text { egg laying mammals }
$$

(i) (ii)
placental mammals
 $$
\text { egg laying mammals }
$$

(i) (ii)
placental mammals
 pouched mammals

explanation:
Marsupials are commonly called “pouched mammals,” and monotremes are known as “egg-laying mammals.”

Hence the correct analogy is:

Marsupials : pouched mammals :: monotremes : egg-laying mammals

That corresponds to Option B.
